doc.otpinput_length_change_desc: Change the number of input fields with the length property.
doc.otpinput_long_desc: Split input fields for entering one-time passwords (OTP) or PIN codes.
doc.otpinput_motion_focus: Provides a pleasant transition where focus automatically moves to the next field (autofocus) with each character entered.
doc.otpinput_scenario_auth: Entering codes during login or password reset.
doc.otpinput_scenario_pay: Entering security codes during high-value payments.
doc.passwordinput_a11y_toggle: Explicitly state the state of the visibility toggle button (show/hide) using aria-label so that the current mode can be recognized by assistive technologies.
doc.passwordinput_anatomy_visibility: Visibility Toggle Button: Toggles between masking (●●●) the input content and displaying it as plain text.
doc.passwordinput_best_practice_1: Always default to the masked (hidden) state; never auto-reveal the password on mount.
doc.passwordinput_best_practice_2: Provide a confirmation field for new-password flows so users can verify their entry before submitting.
doc.passwordinput_choice_input_when: When the field value is not sensitive and masking is unnecessary.
doc.passwordinput_choice_matrix_desc: Use the right secure-entry component based on the sensitivity of the data.
doc.passwordinput_choice_password_when: When users need to enter sensitive credentials with visibility-toggle support.
doc.passwordinput_content_security: For security reasons, always start with 'hidden (masking)' by default.
doc.passwordinput_design_intent: PasswordInput is a component for securely entering sensitive information such as passwords. In addition to the function of masking the entered content, it provides a 'show/hide' toggle function so that users can confirm whether they have entered it correctly, reducing stress caused by entry errors.
doc.passwordinput_long_desc: Enhanced input field specifically for secure password entry with visibility toggle.
doc.passwordinput_motion_toggle: The visibility toggle transitions the input type between 'password' and 'text' instantly. No animation is applied — the change is functional and immediate.
doc.passwordinput_scenario_account: Account Settings: Confirming current passwords or setting new ones.
doc.phoneinput_a11y_desc: The country selector is implemented as a native <select> element with aria-label='Country code', ensuring compatibility with assistive technologies. The phone number input uses aria-labelledby to associate itself with the field label, and aria-invalid when an error is present.
doc.phoneinput_anatomy_country: Country Selector: A native select element overlaid on a flag + dial code preview. Allows the user to pick their country.
doc.phoneinput_anatomy_number: Phone Number Input: A tel-type input for entering the local phone number, excluding the country dial code.
doc.phoneinput_best_practice_1: Pre-select the country based on the user's locale or IP geolocation when possible.
doc.phoneinput_best_practice_2: Show a placeholder that matches the expected format for the selected country (e.g., 090-0000-0000 for Japan).
doc.phoneinput_best_practice_3: Validate on submit rather than on every keystroke to avoid frustrating the user during entry.
doc.phoneinput_choice_input_when: When the phone format is always from a single country and no country selection is needed.
doc.phoneinput_choice_inputmask_when: When you need format enforcement (e.g., (99) 9999-9999) without a country selector.
doc.phoneinput_choice_matrix_desc: Choose based on whether international phone number entry is required.
doc.phoneinput_choice_phoneinput_when: When users need to enter an international phone number with country code selection.
doc.phoneinput_content_guideline_1: Keep placeholder text format-aware for the selected country to guide input.
doc.phoneinput_content_guideline_2: Display the dial code visually alongside the flag to reduce ambiguity.
doc.phoneinput_design_intent: PhoneInput combines a country code selector with a phone number field into a single cohesive input. It solves the common UX problem of collecting international phone numbers by letting users pick their country — displaying the flag and dial code — without needing to manually type the country code.
doc.phoneinput_long_desc: Composite input for international phone numbers with a country dial code selector.
doc.phoneinput_motion_desc: No animation is applied. Selecting a country instantly updates the flag and dial code preview.
doc.phoneinput_scenario_checkout: E-commerce Checkout: Collecting a shipping contact number that may be from any country.
doc.phoneinput_scenario_signup: User Registration: Collecting a mobile number for SMS verification during international sign-up.
doc.radio_a11y_group: Wrap in role="radiogroup" to tell assistive technologies that the choices are related.
doc.radio_a11y_keyboard: Move between items using arrow keys; selection changes automatically.
doc.radio_anatomy_button: The center dot indicating the selection state, mimicking traditional physical buttons.
doc.radio_anatomy_label: The item name. Selectable by clicking the text as well.
doc.radio_choice_matrix_desc: Decide based on the number of choices and whether the user needs to see all options.
doc.radio_choice_radio_when: When there are few choices (5 or fewer) and you want a bird's-eye view of all items.
doc.radio_choice_select_when: When there are many choices (6 or more) and you want to prioritize space saving.
doc.radio_content_consistency: Type labels consistently across all items to make them easier to compare.
doc.radio_design_intent: Radio buttons are used to force a single selection from multiple mutually exclusive choices.
doc.radio_long_desc: Radio buttons allow users to select one option from a set.
doc.radio_motion_pop: Add a subtle scale animation when the center dot appears upon selection to enhance responsiveness.
doc.radio_note_default: It is recommended to set a default value for the most likely choice (e.g., Standard Shipping).
doc.radio_note_layout: Stack options vertically and ensure labels are clickable to improve usability.
doc.radio_scenario_payment: Payment Method Selection: Selecting one from credit card, bank transfer, etc.
doc.radio_scenario_plan: Plan Selection: Upgrading or downgrading a service plan.
doc.radio_title: A control that allows users to choose only one from a set of mutually exclusive options
doc.radio_when_to_use: Best for selecting exactly one option from 3 or more choices. Consider Switch for 2 states (On/Off) or Checkbox for multiple selections.
doc.radiogroup_a11y_group: Use role="radiogroup" and ensure standard behavior where items can be navigated between using keyboard arrow keys.
doc.radiogroup_anatomy_wrapper: A container that groups radio buttons. Can be arranged vertically or horizontally.
doc.radiogroup_best_practice_1: Always preselect a sensible default option; a required group with no default selection forces unnecessary cognitive effort.
doc.radiogroup_best_practice_2: List options in a logical order — most common first, or natural sequence (e.g., S → M → L → XL for sizes).
doc.radiogroup_choice_matrix_desc: Select the optimal component according to the number of choices and the importance of the operation.
doc.radiogroup_choice_radio_when: When there are about 3 to 5 choices and you want to keep all choices exposed at all times.
doc.radiogroup_choice_segmented_when: When you want to operate with a feeling closer to control buttons, such as switching ON/OFF or switching views.
doc.radiogroup_choice_selectbox_when: When there are 5 or more choices and you want to save vertical space.
doc.radiogroup_content_default: To clarify the user's intent, usually select one of the items by default or specify that it is a required field.
doc.radiogroup_design_intent: RadioGroup is a component for grouping multiple exclusive choices (only one can be selected) and managing a single selection state. It visually and structurally clarifies the constraint that the user must select exactly one.
doc.radiogroup_long_desc: RadioGroup manages a set of Radio buttons.
doc.radiogroup_motion_focus: Visually emphasize the currently focused item during tab key navigation to guide keyboard operations.
doc.radiogroup_scenario_gender: Gender Selection: Single selection from limited choices.
doc.radiogroup_scenario_plan: Price Plan: Selecting one from Plan A, B, or C.
doc.radiogroup_title: Management component for grouping related radio buttons
doc.rangecalendar_a11y_desc: Follows WAI-ARIA guidelines with proper roles, labels, and keyboard navigation support.
doc.rangecalendar_anatomy_content: The primary area where main content or children are rendered.
doc.rangecalendar_anatomy_root: The outermost container managing state and layout.
doc.rangecalendar_best_practice_1: Use RangeCalendar consistently across your application to maintain a predictable user experience.
doc.rangecalendar_best_practice_2: Test RangeCalendar behavior across different screen sizes and input methods to ensure accessibility.
doc.rangecalendar_choice_alt_when: When a simpler alternative like DateRangePicker better suits the use case.
doc.rangecalendar_choice_matrix_desc: Select the most appropriate component based on your requirements.
doc.rangecalendar_choice_self_when: When RangeCalendar functionality with standard options is required.
doc.rangecalendar_content_guideline: Keep content concise and relevant to the component's purpose. Follow consistent labeling conventions across your application.
doc.rangecalendar_design_intent: RangeCalendar provides a consistent, reusable pattern for standalone calendar for selecting a date range without text input, reducing implementation effort and ensuring design system compliance.
doc.rangecalendar_motion_desc: Transitions are minimal and purposeful, respecting the user's reduced-motion preferences.
doc.rangecalendar_scenario_1: Integrating RangeCalendar into workflows for data collection or display.
doc.rangecalendar_scenario_2: Using RangeCalendar in management views for information organization.
doc.rangeslider_a11y_desc: Both thumbs are keyboard-navigable with arrow keys. Each thumb has aria-valuemin, aria-valuemax, and aria-valuenow attributes.
doc.rangeslider_best_practice_1: Display the current min and max values near the thumbs or in adjacent inputs for precise control.
doc.rangeslider_best_practice_2: Prevent thumbs from crossing each other to maintain intuitive min-max semantics.
doc.rangeslider_choice_alt_when: When only a single numeric value is needed (use Slider).
doc.rangeslider_choice_matrix_desc: Select based on whether a range or single value is needed.
doc.rangeslider_choice_self_when: When users need to define a numeric range (e.g., price range, date range).
doc.rangeslider_long_desc: A slider component for selecting a range (start and end).
doc.rating_a11y_desc: Supports keyboard navigation with arrow keys to adjust the rating. Uses role='radiogroup' with each star as role='radio' and aria-checked.
doc.rating_a11y_reading: Clearly communicate the current value (e.g., 'Current 4.5 stars') and the maximum value (e.g., 'out of 5 stars') to screen readers.
doc.rating_anatomy_stars: Icons (usually star-shaped) indicating the current rating level.
doc.rating_best_practice_1: Show a textual label alongside the stars (e.g., 'Excellent', 'Poor') to clarify the meaning of each level.
doc.rating_best_practice_2: Consider whether half-star precision is needed — simpler scales (whole stars only) reduce decision fatigue.
doc.rating_choice_alt_when: When detailed textual feedback is needed (use Textarea or a survey form).
doc.rating_choice_matrix_desc: Select based on the type of feedback collection needed.
doc.rating_choice_self_when: When collecting quick ordinal feedback (1-5 stars) for products, services, or experiences.
doc.rating_content_precision: Set appropriately whether to allow input in 0.5 increments (half-stars) depending on the granularity of the evaluation.
doc.rating_design_intent: Rating is a component for users to feedback 'sensory satisfaction' such as a 5-star rating for products or services. It provides a visual UI that is more intuitive than numeric input, allowing evaluation content to be understood at a glance.
doc.rating_disabled_desc: Using the disabled prop makes the component non-interactive and grays it out.
doc.rating_half_desc: Using the allowHalf prop allows for ratings in increments of 0.5.
doc.rating_long_desc: A component for displaying and entering ratings.
doc.rating_motion_hover: Enhance operational responsiveness with a preview effect (hover feedback) where the star color changes on mouseover.
doc.rating_readonly_desc: Using the readOnly prop makes the component display-only. Unlike disabled, the stars retain their normal color — ideal for showing other users' ratings.
doc.rating_scenario_review: Evaluating satisfaction of purchased items with stars.
doc.rating_scenario_survey: Answering the ease of use of a service on a 5-point scale.
doc.rating_size_desc: Supports three sizes: small, medium, and large.
doc.richTextEditor_a11y_desc: The editor area carries role='textbox' and aria-multiline. Each toolbar button has an aria-label and aria-pressed. The toolbar itself has role='toolbar'. Keyboard users can Tab into the editor and use Ctrl+B / Ctrl+I / Ctrl+U for common shortcuts.
doc.richTextEditor_anatomy_editor: A contentEditable region where users type and see live-formatted text.
doc.richTextEditor_anatomy_toolbar: A row of formatting action buttons (bold, italic, headings, lists, link, etc.).
doc.richTextEditor_best_practice_1: Always sanitize the HTML output before rendering — the editor emits raw HTML that could contain injected scripts.
doc.richTextEditor_best_practice_2: Limit the toolbar to the formatting options your content model actually supports; hiding irrelevant buttons reduces cognitive load.
doc.richTextEditor_choice_markdown_when: When the audience is technical and Markdown syntax is preferred over visual formatting.
doc.richTextEditor_choice_matrix_desc: Select the appropriate component based on the expected content type and formatting needs.
doc.richTextEditor_choice_rte_when: When users need to format text (bold, italic, headings, lists, links) and the output is rendered as HTML.
doc.richTextEditor_choice_textarea_when: When only plain text is needed (comments, short descriptions) and formatting is irrelevant.
doc.richTextEditor_content_output: The component outputs HTML strings via the onChange callback. Sanitize the output before rendering to prevent XSS.
doc.richTextEditor_content_placeholder: Use the placeholder prop to hint at the expected content. The placeholder disappears on focus.
doc.richTextEditor_design_intent: RichTextEditor bridges the gap between plain Textarea and complex document editors. It is suited to situations where users need to structure text with headings, lists, bold/italic emphasis, or hyperlinks — without needing to write HTML themselves.
doc.richTextEditor_long_desc: RichTextEditor is a WYSIWYG (What You See Is What You Get) editor that provides a familiar word-processor-like experience for entering and formatting rich text content.
doc.richTextEditor_motion_toolbar: Toolbar buttons show an active (pressed) highlight when the cursor is inside formatted text, giving instant visual feedback.
doc.richTextEditor_scenario_article: Composing articles with headings, paragraphs, and embedded links.
doc.richTextEditor_scenario_comment: Rich-formatted comments or replies where bold and lists improve readability.
doc.richTextEditor_scenario_email: Drafting formatted emails with bullet points and hyperlinks.
doc.richTextEditor_title: WYSIWYG editor for formatted HTML content
doc.richTextEditor_toolbar_desc: The toolbar prop accepts an array of item keys and 'separator' strings, allowing full customization of which buttons appear and in what order.
doc.richTextEditor_variant_desc: RichTextEditor supports the same variant and status props as other form inputs for visual consistency.
doc.searchinput_a11y_desc: Uses role='searchbox' and aria-label. The clear button is keyboard-accessible. Supports live search results announced via aria-live.
doc.searchinput_a11y_label: Even with a visual icon, always set an accessibility label (aria-label="Search") to clearly state the purpose.
doc.searchinput_anatomy_clear: Displayed on the right when there is input, allowing content to be erased with one click.
doc.searchinput_anatomy_icon: Placed on the left, suggesting that the purpose of the field is 'search'.
doc.searchinput_best_practice_1: Include a visible search icon and placeholder text to clearly communicate the input's purpose.
doc.searchinput_best_practice_2: Provide a clear/reset button to allow users to quickly clear the search query.
doc.searchinput_choice_alt_when: When the input is part of a complex filter form (use Input with custom icons).
doc.searchinput_choice_matrix_desc: Select based on the search interaction model needed.
doc.searchinput_choice_self_when: When you need a dedicated search field with clear button and search icon.
doc.searchinput_content_placeholder: Set text that specifically indicates the search target, such as 'Enter keyword' or 'Search server'.
doc.searchinput_design_intent: SearchInput is an input component specialized for searching information. It comes standard with a search icon that communicates 'searching purpose' at a glance and a clear button to quickly discard complex search terms, optimizing the user's discovery experience.
doc.searchinput_long_desc: A component that provides a search interface.
doc.searchinput_motion_clear: A fade effect is applied to the appearance and disappearance of the clear button to communicate UI changes naturally.
doc.searchinput_scenario_global: Site-wide search placed in the header.
doc.searchinput_scenario_list: Search placed at the top of a table or list for item extraction.
doc.searchinput_title: A text input field specifically designed for search queries, including a search icon.
doc.selectbox_a11y_1: Proper use of role="combobox" and role="listbox"
doc.selectbox_a11y_2: State management for aria-expanded, aria-selected, and aria-disabled
doc.selectbox_a11y_desc: The Selectbox component complies with WAI-ARIA accessibility guidelines:
doc.selectbox_a11y_listbox: Uses role='listbox' and indicates the currently selected item with aria-selected.
doc.selectbox_a11y_scroll: Automatically scrolls so that the focused item is always within the display area during keyboard operation.
doc.selectbox_adv_comb_desc: Search and grouping can be used simultaneously. Only groups containing options that match the search criteria will be displayed.
doc.selectbox_adv_search_desc: Allows users to search through options. Useful when there are many choices.
doc.selectbox_anatomy_menu: The floating area that displays the options (Options).
doc.selectbox_anatomy_search: The input field for finding items among a large number of choices.
doc.selectbox_anatomy_trigger: The area that displays the current value and expands the menu on click.
doc.selectbox_best_practice_1: If there are more than 10 options, enable Searchable Select functionality to reduce the user's search burden.
doc.selectbox_best_practice_2: If there are many related items, use Grouped options to organize them.
doc.selectbox_choice_combobox_when: When there are a very large number of options and the user wants to narrow them down by free entry or search.
doc.selectbox_choice_matrix_desc: Select the most appropriate input format based on the number of choices and screen space.
doc.selectbox_choice_radio_when: When there are few options (5 or fewer) and you want to show all options at once.
doc.selectbox_choice_select_when: When there are 6 or more options, or when you want to save space.
doc.selectbox_content_labels: Keep labels in the list concise and ideally fitting on a single line.
doc.selectbox_content_placeholder: Set text like 'Select...' that prompts the user for the next action.
doc.selectbox_custom_filter_desc: By default, a case-insensitive partial match search is performed, but you can also provide a custom filter function.
doc.selectbox_desc: A dropdown menu for selecting one option from a predefined list
doc.selectbox_design_intent: Selectboxes are components used to accurately select one option from a large number of choices within a limited display space. By hiding choices in the default state, they reduce screen complexity.
doc.selectbox_guideline_1: Use when there are many options and space is insufficient for RadioGroups or SegmentedControls.
doc.selectbox_guideline_2: If there are 5 or fewer options, consider using highly visible RadioButtons or SegmentedControls.
doc.selectbox_long_desc: A dropdown menu for users to select one option from a predefined set.
doc.selectbox_motion_opacity: Smoothly displays the menu with a combination of fade and subtle slide when expanded.
doc.selectbox_op_first: Focus first option with Home key
doc.selectbox_op_last: Focus last option with End key
doc.selectbox_op_next: Focus next option with down arrow key
doc.selectbox_op_prev: Focus previous option with up arrow key
doc.selectbox_scenario_country: Country Selection: For 200+ options, a selectbox with search functionality is ideal.
doc.selectbox_scenario_form: Profile Settings: A space-saving selection method when there are many items on the screen.
doc.selectbox_title: A dropdown menu for selecting one option from a predefined list
doc.selectbox_ts_desc: The Selectbox component provides full type definitions.
doc.selectbox_var_native_desc: Enables the browser-standard `<select>` element. In modern browsers (e.g., Chrome 119+), separators are supported within native selects using `<hr>`. Options with `type: "separator"` will be rendered as `<hr>`.
doc.selectbox_var_sep_desc: A dividing line can be displayed between options by specifying `type: "separator"`.
doc.signaturepad_a11y_desc: Follows WAI-ARIA guidelines with proper roles, labels, and keyboard navigation support.
doc.signaturepad_anatomy_content: The primary area where main content or children are rendered.
doc.signaturepad_anatomy_root: The outermost container managing state and layout.
doc.signaturepad_best_practice_1: Use SignaturePad consistently across your application to maintain a predictable user experience.
doc.signaturepad_best_practice_2: Test SignaturePad behavior across different screen sizes and input methods to ensure accessibility.
doc.signaturepad_choice_alt_when: When a simpler alternative like Canvas better suits the use case.
doc.signaturepad_choice_matrix_desc: Select the most appropriate component based on your requirements.
doc.signaturepad_choice_self_when: When SignaturePad functionality with standard options is required.
doc.signaturepad_content_guideline: Keep content concise and relevant to the component's purpose. Follow consistent labeling conventions across your application.
doc.signaturepad_design_intent: SignaturePad provides a consistent, reusable pattern for capturing handwritten signatures digitally, reducing implementation effort and ensuring design system compliance.
doc.signaturepad_long_desc: SignaturePad is a component for entering handwritten signatures via mouse or touch.
doc.signaturepad_motion_desc: Transitions are minimal and purposeful, respecting the user's reduced-motion preferences.
doc.signaturepad_scenario_1: Integrating SignaturePad into workflows for data collection or display.
doc.signaturepad_scenario_2: Using SignaturePad in management views for information organization.
doc.signaturepad_var_color_desc: You can change the color and thickness of the pen (signature).
doc.slider_a11y_desc: The thumb is keyboard-navigable with arrow keys. Uses role='slider' with aria-valuemin, aria-valuemax, and aria-valuenow.
doc.slider_a11y_keyboard: Ensure values can be increased or decreased by fixed amounts using the left/right (up/down) arrow keys.
doc.slider_anatomy_thumb: A circular handle that the user drags to change the value.
doc.slider_anatomy_track: A line indicating the range of value movement. Colors are separated between selected and unselected ranges.
doc.slider_basic_desc: Standard implementation for selecting a single numeric value from a range.
doc.slider_best_practice_1: Display the current value near the thumb or in a tooltip for precise feedback.
doc.slider_best_practice_2: Define appropriate step sizes to balance precision with ease of use.
doc.slider_choice_matrix_desc: Select according to the nature of the value being selected and the operational precision expected by the user.
doc.slider_choice_number_when: When you want to specify a concrete numerical value precisely in single units, such as age or quantity.
doc.slider_choice_slider_when: When sensory adjustment is more suitable than strict numerical values, such as volume, brightness, or price range.
doc.slider_content_labels: Display labels for the minimum and maximum values to clarify what is being adjusted.
doc.slider_custom_marks_desc: Labels and tick marks can be displayed at specific values using the `marks` property.
doc.slider_desc: An input to capture a numeric value from a specified range by dragging a knob
doc.slider_design_intent: Sliders are components for selecting a numerical value between a minimum and maximum value through intuitive drag operations. They are ideal when 'relative position' within a range or 'sensory adjustment' is prioritized over precise numerical input.
doc.slider_long_desc: Details and usage for the Slider component.
doc.slider_motion_slide: The track color changes smoothly following the movement of the knob, providing dynamic feedback of the current value.
doc.slider_range_desc: Use multiple knobs to specify a range of start and end values.
doc.slider_scenario_filter: Narrowing down a specific price range from a product list.
doc.slider_step_desc: Restrict the numeric increment (step size) using the `step` property.
doc.slider_title: Input to capture a numeric value from a specified range by dragging a knob
doc.slider_usage_desc: Select a numeric value intuitively from the set range by dragging the knob.
doc.slider_vertical_desc: Align the slider vertically using the `vertical` property.
doc.switch_a11y_keyboard: The Space key can be used to toggle on/off.
doc.switch_a11y_role: Uses role="switch" to inform screen readers that this is a switch control.
doc.switch_anatomy_thumb: The circular handle that slides along the track to reflect the current state.
doc.switch_anatomy_track: The background element that visually indicates the on/off state via color change.
doc.switch_best_practice_1: Use a Switch only for settings that take effect immediately — if the change requires a form submission, use a Checkbox instead.
doc.switch_best_practice_2: Label the Switch with a noun phrase describing the feature being toggled (e.g., 'Dark mode'), not a verb phrase like 'Enable dark mode'.
doc.switch_choice_check_when: When the change takes effect only after a form submission or confirmation.
doc.switch_choice_matrix_desc: Choose the most appropriate component based on when the change takes effect.
doc.switch_choice_switch_when: When toggling a feature immediately changes the application state (e.g. dark mode, notifications).
doc.switch_content_labels: Accompany the switch with a label that describes what will happen when the state changes (e.g. Receive notifications).
doc.switch_desc: A binary switch for instantly toggling settings between on and off states
doc.switch_design_intent: A Switch is used when the user needs to toggle a single setting that takes effect immediately. Unlike a Checkbox, a Switch implies immediate action without requiring a submit button.
doc.switch_long_desc: Switches are used to toggle the state of a single setting on or off.
doc.switch_motion_slide: The thumb slides to reproduce the physical feel of a real switch, emphasizing the tactile feedback of toggling.
doc.switch_scenario_filtering: Filtering: Toggling specific conditions on/off (e.g. In-stock items only).
doc.switch_scenario_setting: Settings Screen: Toggling notification settings, dark mode, etc.
doc.switch_scenario_settings: Settings Screen: Toggling notification settings or privacy settings.
doc.switch_title: Instantly toggle a single setting on or off
doc.switchgroup_a11y_desc: Each switch in the group is keyboard-togglable. The group uses role='group' with an aria-label describing the collection.
doc.switchgroup_a11y_list: Groups switches together using list markup or role="group" to clarify their logical relationship to screen readers.
doc.switchgroup_anatomy_items: Individual settings consisting of a toggle switch and a descriptive label.
doc.switchgroup_anatomy_label: A title that indicates the overall category of settings.
doc.switchgroup_best_practice_1: Clearly label each switch with a descriptive label so users understand what they are toggling.
doc.switchgroup_best_practice_2: Consider grouping related toggles under a fieldset with a legend for better semantic structure.
doc.switchgroup_choice_alt_when: When a single binary toggle is needed (use Switch) or when selecting from a list (use CheckboxGroup).
doc.switchgroup_choice_matrix_desc: Select based on whether toggles should be grouped or independent.
doc.switchgroup_choice_self_when: When managing multiple related on/off toggles together (e.g., notification preferences).
doc.switchgroup_content_immediate: Since switches often imply immediate reflection, ensure that the UI clearly communicates that each setting has been updated.
doc.switchgroup_design_intent: SwitchGroup is a component for managing multiple related toggle switches collectively. It is suitable for control panels where users need to turn multiple optional features or types of notifications on or off.
doc.switchgroup_long_desc: A group of switch components used to handle multiple toggle selections.
doc.switchgroup_motion_active: When a switch is toggled, visual feedback is provided immediately through color and position changes.
doc.switchgroup_scenario_notify: Toggling different types of notifications (Email, SMS, Push).
doc.textarea_a11y_desc: When entering long sentences, clarify the focus state so that users do not lose sight of their entry position, and communicate entry limits etc. with aria-describedby as needed.
doc.textarea_anatomy_field: An editable area that accepts multiple lines of text.
doc.textarea_anatomy_resize: A part that allows users to manually adjust the height (browser function or style setting).
doc.textarea_best_practice_1: Set a reasonable default height and allow resizing so users can accommodate varying text lengths.
doc.textarea_best_practice_2: Show character count or limit when there is a maximum length to help users manage their input.
doc.textarea_choice_input_when: When entering short, single pieces of information such as names, addresses (partial), or phone numbers.
doc.textarea_choice_matrix_desc: Select according to the amount and quality of information expected to be entered.
doc.textarea_choice_textarea_when: When entry of sentences of several lines or more is anticipated (e.g., contact details).
doc.textarea_content_prefill: If the entry content is unclear, present concrete description examples as placeholders or help text.
doc.textarea_design_intent: Textarea is a component for enabling entry of long-form plain text such as descriptions, comments, or messages. It provides a wider entry area than a standard Input and increases the degree of freedom in writing by supporting scrolling and (where necessary) automatic resizing.
doc.textarea_motion_expand: If there is an auto-size (automatic resizing) function, height changes smoothly with entry, eliminating inconvenience during writing.
doc.textarea_scenario_comment: Entering long comments for photos or videos.
doc.textarea_scenario_contact: Sending question content in a free-form description format.
doc.textarea_title: Multi-line text field optimized for long-form input
doc.timepicker_a11y_desc: Follows WAI-ARIA guidelines with proper roles, labels, and keyboard navigation support.
doc.timepicker_anatomy_content: The primary area where main content or children are rendered.
doc.timepicker_anatomy_root: The outermost container managing state and layout.
doc.timepicker_best_practice_1: Use TimePicker consistently across your application to maintain a predictable user experience.
doc.timepicker_best_practice_2: Test TimePicker behavior across different screen sizes and input methods to ensure accessibility.
doc.timepicker_choice_alt_when: When a simpler alternative like DatePicker better suits the use case.
doc.timepicker_choice_matrix_desc: Select the most appropriate component based on your requirements.
doc.timepicker_choice_self_when: When TimePicker functionality with standard options is required.
doc.timepicker_content_guideline: Keep content concise and relevant to the component's purpose. Follow consistent labeling conventions across your application.
doc.timepicker_desc: A component for users to select time. It wraps the native <input type="time"> and adjusts the styling.
doc.timepicker_design_intent: TimePicker provides a consistent, reusable pattern for time selection input with hour and minute controls, reducing implementation effort and ensuring design system compliance.
doc.timepicker_motion_desc: Transitions are minimal and purposeful, respecting the user's reduced-motion preferences.
doc.timepicker_scenario_1: Integrating TimePicker into workflows for data collection or display.
doc.timepicker_scenario_2: Using TimePicker in management views for information organization.
doc.timepicker_title: A specialized input for selecting specific hours and minutes
doc.transfer_controlled_desc: You can manage the internal state to synchronize item movement.
doc.transfer_custom_desc: You can set custom titles for the left and right columns.
doc.transfer_key_arrow_down: Move focus to next item (wraps around)
doc.transfer_key_arrow_up: Move focus to previous item (wraps around)
doc.transfer_key_enter: Move all checked items to the other list
doc.transfer_key_tab: Move focus between lists and operation buttons
doc.transfer_long_desc: Transfer is a component for selecting items by moving them between two columns.
doc.transfer_a11y_desc: Follows WAI-ARIA guidelines with proper roles, labels, and keyboard navigation support.
doc.transfer_anatomy_content: The primary area where main content or children are rendered.
doc.transfer_anatomy_root: The outermost container managing state and layout.
doc.transfer_best_practice_1: Use Transfer consistently across your application to maintain a predictable user experience.
doc.transfer_best_practice_2: Test Transfer behavior across different screen sizes and input methods to ensure accessibility.
doc.transfer_choice_alt_when: When a simpler alternative like MultiSelect better suits the use case.
doc.transfer_choice_matrix_desc: Select the most appropriate component based on your requirements.
doc.transfer_choice_self_when: When Transfer functionality with standard options is required.
doc.transfer_content_guideline: Keep content concise and relevant to the component's purpose. Follow consistent labeling conventions across your application.
doc.transfer_design_intent: Transfer provides a consistent, reusable pattern for dual-list interface for moving items between collections, reducing implementation effort and ensuring design system compliance.
doc.transfer_motion_desc: Transitions are minimal and purposeful, respecting the user's reduced-motion preferences.
doc.transfer_scenario_1: Integrating Transfer into workflows for data collection or display.
doc.transfer_scenario_2: Using Transfer in management views for information organization.
doc.treeselect_a11y_desc: Follows WAI-ARIA guidelines with proper roles, labels, and keyboard navigation support.
doc.treeselect_anatomy_content: The primary area where main content or children are rendered.
doc.treeselect_anatomy_root: The outermost container managing state and layout.
doc.treeselect_best_practice_1: Use TreeSelect consistently across your application to maintain a predictable user experience.
doc.treeselect_best_practice_2: Test TreeSelect behavior across different screen sizes and input methods to ensure accessibility.
doc.treeselect_choice_alt_when: When a simpler alternative like Cascader better suits the use case.
doc.treeselect_choice_matrix_desc: Select the most appropriate component based on your requirements.
doc.treeselect_choice_self_when: When TreeSelect functionality with standard options is required.
doc.treeselect_content_guideline: Keep content concise and relevant to the component's purpose. Follow consistent labeling conventions across your application.
doc.treeselect_design_intent: TreeSelect provides a consistent, reusable pattern for tree-structured dropdown for hierarchical data selection, reducing implementation effort and ensuring design system compliance.
doc.treeselect_long_desc: TreeSelect is a component for selecting one or more items from a tree-like list.
doc.treeselect_motion_desc: Transitions are minimal and purposeful, respecting the user's reduced-motion preferences.
doc.treeselect_multi_desc: In multi-selection mode, you can select multiple nodes using checkboxes.
doc.treeselect_scenario_1: Integrating TreeSelect into workflows for data collection or display.
doc.treeselect_scenario_2: Using TreeSelect in management views for information organization.
doc.daterangepicker_scenario_booking: Selecting check-in/check-out dates or reservation periods.
doc.daterangepicker_scenario_report: Defining report date ranges for analytics or data exports.
doc.checkbox_keyboard_desc: Tab focuses the checkbox. Space toggles the checked state. Disabled checkboxes are skipped during keyboard navigation.
doc.checkbox_i18n_desc: All visible text uses i18n keys. Pass translated strings through the label prop or children.
doc.checkboxgroup_keyboard_desc: Tab moves focus to the checkbox group. Space toggles individual checkboxes. Arrow keys move between options.
doc.checkboxgroup_i18n_desc: All visible text uses i18n keys. Pass translated strings through option labels and the group legend.
doc.colorinput_keyboard_desc: Tab focuses the color input field. Standard keyboard input is supported for hex values. Escape cancels editing.
doc.colorinput_i18n_desc: All visible text uses i18n keys. Pass translated strings through the label and placeholder props.
doc.colorpicker_keyboard_desc: Tab moves focus within the color picker. Arrow keys adjust hue and saturation values. Escape closes the picker.
doc.colorpicker_i18n_desc: All visible text uses i18n keys. Pass translated strings through the label and aria-label props.
doc.combobox_keyboard_desc: Tab focuses the combobox. Arrow keys navigate options. Enter selects the highlighted option. Escape closes the dropdown.
doc.combobox_i18n_desc: All visible text uses i18n keys. Pass translated strings through the label, placeholder, and option label props.
doc.datagrid_keyboard_desc: Tab moves focus between interactive elements. Arrow keys navigate cells. Enter activates cell actions. Escape cancels editing.
doc.datagrid_i18n_desc: All visible text uses i18n keys. Pass translated strings through column header and cell content props.
doc.datepicker_keyboard_desc: Tab focuses the date input field. Arrow keys navigate the calendar. Enter selects a date. Escape closes the calendar.
doc.datepicker_i18n_desc: All visible text uses i18n keys. Pass translated strings through the placeholder and aria-label props, or override via the i18n namespace.
doc.daterangepicker_keyboard_desc: Tab moves between start and end date fields. Arrow keys navigate the calendar. Enter selects dates. Escape closes the calendar.
doc.daterangepicker_i18n_desc: All visible text uses i18n keys. Pass translated strings through the placeholder and label props, or override via the i18n namespace.
doc.input_keyboard_desc: Tab focuses the field. Standard keyboard input is supported. Escape clears the field when clearable is enabled.
doc.input_i18n_desc: All visible text uses i18n keys. Pass translated strings through the label, placeholder, and helper text props.
doc.inputmask_keyboard_desc: Tab focuses the masked input field. Standard keyboard input is supported within the mask pattern. Escape cancels where applicable.
doc.inputmask_i18n_desc: All visible text uses i18n keys. Pass translated strings through the label and placeholder props.
doc.inputgroup_keyboard_desc: Tab moves focus through each element in the group sequentially. Standard keyboard interaction applies to each individual input.
doc.inputgroup_i18n_desc: All visible text uses i18n keys. Pass translated strings through the label and placeholder props of each input within the group.
doc.multiselect_keyboard_desc: Tab focuses the multiselect. Arrow keys navigate options. Enter selects or deselects. Escape closes the dropdown. Backspace removes the last selected tag.
doc.multiselect_i18n_desc: All visible text uses i18n keys. Pass translated strings through the label, placeholder, and option label props.
doc.radio_keyboard_desc: Tab focuses the radio button. Arrow keys move between options within the group, automatically selecting each option.
doc.radio_i18n_desc: All visible text uses i18n keys. Pass translated strings through the label prop or children.
doc.radiogroup_keyboard_desc: Tab focuses the radio group. Arrow keys navigate and select between options within the group.
doc.radiogroup_i18n_desc: All visible text uses i18n keys. Pass translated strings through option labels and the group legend.
doc.rangeslider_keyboard_desc: Tab focuses each slider thumb. Arrow keys adjust the value. Home and End move to min and max values respectively.
doc.rangeslider_i18n_desc: All visible text uses i18n keys. Pass translated strings through the aria-label prop for each thumb.
doc.selectbox_keyboard_desc: Tab focuses the select. Enter or Space opens the dropdown. Arrow keys navigate options. Escape closes without selection.
doc.selectbox_i18n_desc: All visible text uses i18n keys. Pass translated strings through option labels and the placeholder prop.
doc.slider_keyboard_desc: Tab focuses the slider thumb. Arrow keys adjust the value incrementally. Home and End move to min and max values.
doc.slider_i18n_desc: All visible text uses i18n keys. Pass translated strings through the aria-label and aria-valuetext props.
doc.switch_keyboard_desc: Tab focuses the switch. Space toggles the on/off state. Disabled switches are not reachable by keyboard.
doc.switch_i18n_desc: All visible text uses i18n keys. Pass translated strings through the label prop or children.
doc.switchgroup_keyboard_desc: Tab moves focus to the switch group. Space toggles individual switches. Arrow keys move between options.
doc.switchgroup_i18n_desc: All visible text uses i18n keys. Pass translated strings through option labels and the group legend.
doc.textarea_keyboard_desc: Tab focuses the textarea field. Standard keyboard input including Enter for new lines is supported. Escape cancels where applicable.
doc.textarea_i18n_desc: All visible text uses i18n keys. Pass translated strings through the label and placeholder props.
doc.timepicker_keyboard_desc: Tab focuses the time input field. Arrow keys adjust hours and minutes. Escape closes the time picker panel.
doc.timepicker_i18n_desc: All visible text uses i18n keys. Pass translated strings through the placeholder and aria-label props.
doc.treeselect_keyboard_desc: Tab focuses the tree select. Arrow keys navigate tree nodes. Right arrow expands a node; Left arrow collapses it. Enter selects a node.
doc.treeselect_i18n_desc: All visible text uses i18n keys. Pass translated strings through the placeholder and node label props.
doc.cascader_keyboard_desc: Tab focuses the cascader. Arrow keys navigate options at each level. Right arrow expands a submenu; Left arrow collapses it. Enter selects the focused option. Escape closes the dropdown.
doc.cascader_i18n_desc: All visible text uses i18n keys. Pass translated strings through the placeholder and option label props.
doc.fielderror_keyboard_desc: This component does not require specific keyboard interaction. It is announced to screen readers via aria-live or aria-describedby.
doc.fielderror_i18n_desc: All visible text uses i18n keys. Pass translated strings through the children prop.
doc.fieldset_keyboard_desc: Tab moves focus through the form elements contained within the fieldset in DOM order.
doc.fieldset_i18n_desc: All visible text uses i18n keys. Pass translated strings through the legend and children props.
doc.fileupload_keyboard_desc: Tab focuses the upload area. Enter or Space opens the file picker. Keyboard activation is supported as an alternative to drag-and-drop.
doc.fileupload_i18n_desc: All visible text uses i18n keys. Pass translated strings through the label and description props.
doc.numberinput_keyboard_desc: Tab focuses the input. Arrow Up increments the value; Arrow Down decrements it. Home moves to the minimum value; End moves to the maximum. Standard keyboard input is also supported.
doc.numberinput_i18n_desc: All visible text uses i18n keys. Pass translated strings through the placeholder and aria-label props.
doc.otpinput_keyboard_desc: Each digit field is individually focusable. Tab moves between fields. After entering a digit, focus advances automatically to the next field. Backspace clears the current field and moves focus back.
doc.otpinput_i18n_desc: All visible text uses i18n keys. Pass translated strings through the aria-label prop.
doc.passwordinput_keyboard_desc: Tab focuses the password field. Standard keyboard input is supported. Tab again reaches the visibility toggle button; Enter or Space shows or hides the password.
doc.passwordinput_i18n_desc: All visible text uses i18n keys. Pass translated strings through the placeholder and aria-label props.
doc.phoneinput_keyboard_desc: Tab focuses the country code selector first, then the phone number input. Arrow keys navigate country options in the dropdown. Standard keyboard input is supported in the number field.
doc.phoneinput_i18n_desc: All visible text uses i18n keys. Pass translated strings through the placeholder and aria-label props.
doc.rating_keyboard_desc: Tab focuses the rating component. Arrow Left decreases the rating; Arrow Right increases it. Home sets the minimum; End sets the maximum. Enter or Space confirms the selection.
doc.rating_i18n_desc: All visible text uses i18n keys. Pass translated strings through the aria-label prop.
doc.richtexteditor_keyboard_desc: Tab focuses the editor area. Standard keyboard shortcuts apply for editing (Ctrl+B bold, Ctrl+I italic, etc.). Tab moves focus to the toolbar when inside the editor. Arrow keys navigate toolbar buttons.
doc.richtexteditor_i18n_desc: All visible text uses i18n keys. Pass translated strings through the placeholder and toolbar aria-label props.
doc.searchinput_keyboard_desc: Tab focuses the search field. Standard keyboard input is supported. Enter submits the search. Escape clears the field when clearable is enabled.
doc.searchinput_i18n_desc: All visible text uses i18n keys. Pass translated strings through the placeholder and aria-label props.
doc.signaturepad_keyboard_desc: This component is primarily designed for mouse and touch interaction. The clear button is reachable via Tab and activated with Enter or Space.
doc.signaturepad_i18n_desc: All visible text uses i18n keys. Pass translated strings through the aria-label and clear button label props.
